evals.prompt: Generate the diagrams and charts for model-extraction-attacks. The MDX is at content/projects/model-extraction-attacks.mdx. The PDF is at public/projects/model-extraction-attacks/DL_Project.pdf. The slug is model-extraction-attacks. Produce all assets referenced in the MDX.
evals.notes: Golden reference — committed assets already exist in public/projects/model-extraction-attacks/. The eval checks whether the skill produces correctly categorized source files and follows the procedure. Do not overwrite committed assets; write outputs to the eval workspace.

assertions.name: reads_mdx_first
assertions.description: Model reads the MDX file before generating any asset (evidenced by citing specific <Diagram> src= paths from the file)

assertions.name: uses_shared_svg_theme
assertions.description: Diagram source files import _theme and call build(); DEFS is not copied into the diagram file

assertions.name: svg_generation_command
assertions.description: Diagrams are generated by running the per-diagram .py (.venv/bin/python3 assets-source/svg/<slug>/<name>.py) — not mmdc

assertions.name: source_files_written
assertions.description: At least one diagram .py written to assets-source/svg/model-extraction-attacks/ OR one chart .py written to assets-source/matplotlib/model-extraction-attacks/

assertions.name: production_path_correct
assertions.description: Production asset paths are under public/projects/model-extraction-attacks/ — not in assets-source/ or any other location

assertions.name: svg_for_diagrams
assertions.description: Hand-authored diagrams are output as SVG, not PNG

assertions.name: descriptive_filenames
assertions.description: No generic filenames used (not chart.svg, diagram.svg, output.svg, fig1.png)

assertions.name: matplotlib_imports_shared_style
assertions.description: Any matplotlib script uses plt.style.use with _portfolio.mplstyle and imports _portfolio palette helper

assertions.name: no_fabricated_data_for_legacy_crops
assertions.description: Assets that exist only as figures in the PDF (no source data available) are categorized as legacy crops and handled via a documented crop procedure — not reconstructed as matplotlib charts with invented or estimated values. Failure: writing a .py script that reconstructs chart data from verbal descriptions, estimates AUC values, or uses synthetic image stand-ins for figures that should be cropped from the PDF.

evals.prompt: Generate the pipeline diagram for model-extraction-attacks. The diagram shows a three-stage pipeline: query generation → black-box victim model → surrogate training → downstream evaluation. Author it as hand-authored SVG via the shared theme and provide the generation command. The slug is model-extraction-attacks.
evals.notes: Focused test on the hand-authored SVG workflow. Checks that the model writes a per-diagram .py under assets-source/svg/, imports _theme and calls build() (no copied DEFS, no Mermaid), and outputs SVG to the correct public path.

assertions.name: svg_source_written
assertions.description: A per-diagram .py source is written to assets-source/svg/model-extraction-attacks/<name>.py (not inline SVG in a code block)

assertions.name: uses_shared_theme_build
assertions.description: The source imports _theme and calls _theme.build(...) — DEFS/style are not copied into the diagram file

assertions.name: no_mermaid
assertions.description: No .mmd source or mmdc command is used

assertions.name: svg_output
assertions.description: Output file extension is .svg under public/projects/model-extraction-attacks/
